analysis_hints: Low alignment (3%) — agent and human disagree more than half the time. What strategy does the human have that the agent is missing?
High-confidence disagreement rate = 97%. Agent is SURE about an action the human avoids. These are the most valuable disagreements — the agent has learned a wrong confident habit.
Disagreement near terminal/death = 86%. Agent diverges from human exactly when survival matters most. Suggest: elevate near_death transitions in priority buffer.
High mean entropy (2.05) — agent is uncertain across the board. Imitation training may need more steps, or the action space is too large for current training data volume.

inbox_schema.description: Respond with a JSON array of hypothesis operations.

priority_boost.description: Boost replay priority for transitions matching condition

imitation_weight.description: Scale imitation loss contribution for specific action

explore_suppress.description: Reduce epsilon for specific human actions the agent avoids

instructions: You are Tetra analyzing Atari human-play vs agent alignment data. For each game, you receive: alignment_rate, high_conf_disagree_rate, disagree_near_terminal_rate, mean_entropy, calibration_proxy, reward_on_agree/disagree, top human actions, top disagreement actions, and benchmark comparison (baseline RL vs human-seeded RL). Write 2-4 focused hypothesis operations per game. Reference specific numbers. Output ONLY a valid JSON array.
